back_translation: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
'Does a wise man answer with empty knowledge, or fill his belly with the hot east wind?
Should he argue with useless words, or with speech that accomplishes nothing?
But you even undermine the fear of God and hinder meditation before Him.
For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.
Your own mouth condemns you, not I; your own lips testify against you.
Were you the first man to be born? Were you brought forth before the hills?
Have you listened in the secret council of God, and do you limit wisdom to yourself?
What do you know that we do not? What do you understand that is not clear to us?
Both the gray-haired and the aged are on our side, men older than your father.
Are the consolations of God not enough for you, even words spoken gently to you?
Why has your heart carried you away, and why do your eyes flash,
that you turn your spirit against God and pour out such words from your mouth?
What is man, that he should be pure? How can one born of woman be righteous?
Behold, even His holy ones He does not trust, and the heavens are not pure in His eyes;
how much more man, who is vile and corrupt, who drinks injustice like water!
Listen to me, and I will tell you; I will recount what I have seen,
what wise men have declared, and did not conceal from their fathers,
to whom alone the land was given, when no foreigner passed among them.
The wicked man writhes in pain all his days, and a few years are reserved for the ruthless.
Sounds of terror ring in his ears; in his prosperity the destroyer attacks him.
He despairs of escaping the darkness; he is marked out for the sword.
He wanders about as food for vultures; he knows the day of darkness is near at hand.
Distress and anguish terrify him; they overwhelm him like a king ready to attack,
for he has stretched out his hand against God and vaunted himself against the Almighty,
rushing at Him stubbornly, with a thick, studded shield.
Though his face is covered with fat and his waist bulges with flesh,
he will dwell in ruined cities, in abandoned houses destined to become heaps of rubble.
He will no longer be rich; his wealth will not endure, and his possessions will not spread over the land.
He will not escape the darkness; a flame will wither his shoots, and he will be swept away by the breath of His mouth.
Let him not deceive himself by trusting in emptiness, for emptiness will be his reward.
It will be paid in full before his time, and his branch will not be green.
He will be like a vine whose unripe grapes are stripped off, like an olive tree that sheds its blossoms.
For the company of the godless will be barren, and fire will consume the tents of bribery.
They conceive trouble and give birth to evil; their womb nurtures deceit.'
